title: Causal relationship between epigenetic markers and type 2 diabetes in West African populations: a Mendelian randomisation analysis
excerpt: This Mendelian randomisation study investigates causal relationships between epigenetic markers and type 2 diabetes risk in West African populations. Using genetic variation as instrumental variables, the analysis aims to determine whether epigenetic modifications have a causal role in type 2 diabetes susceptibility rather than merely being associated with the disease. The research addresses a gap in understanding diabetes pathogenesis in African ancestry populations.

title: AI may be approaching a new phase in healthcare, on two fronts

title: Seqwin: Ultrafast identification of signature sequences in microbial genomes
excerpt: MotivationPolymerase chain reaction (PCR) enables rapid, cost-effective diagnostics but requires prior identification of genomic regions that allow sensitive and specific detection of target microbial groups, herein referred to as microbial signature sequences. We introduce Seqwin, an open-source framework designed to automate microbial genome signature discovery. Tens of thousands of microbial genomes are now available for a single species, limiting the application of existing manual and automated approaches for identifying signatures. Modern approaches that are capable of leveraging all avai

title: Reinforcement Learning for Antibiotic Stewardship: Optimizing Prescribing Policies Under Antimicrobial Resistance Dynamics
excerpt: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) threatens antibiotic effectiveness, but quantitatively evaluating stewardship strategies under partial observability and delayed feedback remains difficult in real-world data. We developed abx_amr_simulator, a Gymnasium-compatible simulation framework, and used it to benchmark reinforcement learning (RL) prescribing policies against value-iteration (VI) benchmarks and fixed prescribing rules across four experiment sets of increasing complexity, with varying levels of information degradation. Across scenarios, temporal abstraction was consistently important: flat

title: Revealing a Correlation between Structure and in vitro Activity of mRNA Lipid Nanoparticles
excerpt: mRNA-loaded lipid nanoparticles (mRNA-LNPs) represent a promising platform for disease prevention, cancer immunotherapy, and gene editing, etc. Despite the success of mRNA-LNPs based vaccines, the relationship between their structure and efficacy remains elusive. Here, we generated a series of mRNA-LNPs with varied structural properties and in vitro cellular activities by altering their processing and storage conditions to investigate the structure-activity relationship (SAR). Our findings revealed a moderate anticorrelation between particle size distribution and in vitro activity. Importantly

title: FDA Launches New Adverse Event Look-Up Tool
excerpt: FDA launched a new unified platform for analyzing adverse event reports. This platform — called the FDA Adverse Event Monitoring System (AEMS) — represents a major achievement in the agency’s mission to modernize and provide radical transparency into the safety of regulated products.

title: FDA Approves First Treatment for Patients with Cerebral Folate Transport Deficiency
excerpt: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved expanded use of Wellcovorin (leucovorin calcium) tablets for the treatment of cerebral folate deficiency in adult and pediatric patients who have a confirmed variant in the folate receptor 1 gene (CFD-FOLR1).

title: FDA Holds Meeting with States on Importation of Lower Cost Drugs
excerpt: FDA held a meeting with several states to discuss the section 804 importation program (SIP), which allows states and Indian tribes to import certain prescription drugs from Canada to significantly reduce the cost of these drugs to the American consumer.

title: Discussions on the generalization of HybridSP on more equivalent benchmarks
excerpt: This article examines the generalization performance of HybridSP across multiple equivalent benchmark datasets. The work evaluates whether HybridSP maintains consistent performance characteristics when applied to comparable evaluation standards, relevant for assessing the robustness and broad applicability of this method in computational or analytical applications.

title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
excerpt: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code , Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month depending on usage — has sparked a growing rebellion among the very programmers it aims to serve. Now, a free alternative is gaining traction. Goose , an open-source AI agent developed by Block (the financial technology company formerly known as Square), offers nearly identical functional

title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
excerpt: Nous Research , the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm , released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four days using 48 of Nvidia's latest B200 graphics processors . title: Recent advances in computational strategies for allosteric site prediction: Machine learning, molecular dynamics, and network-based approaches
excerpt: Recent computational advances enable prediction of allosteric binding sites on proteins using machine learning, molecular dynamics simulations, and network-based approaches. These strategies represent significant progress in identifying alternative drug binding sites beyond the traditional active site, potentially expanding therapeutic options. The integration of computational methods offers improved accuracy and efficiency for allosteric site discovery in drug development.

title: Integrating Machine Learning into Free Energy Perturbation Workflows
excerpt: This study explores the integration of machine learning techniques into free energy perturbation (FEP) workflows, which are established computational methods for predicting protein-ligand binding affinities in drug design. By combining machine learning with FEP, the researchers aim to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of binding affinity predictions in structure-based drug discovery.

title: Artificial intelligence revolution in drug discovery: A paradigm shift in pharmaceutical innovation
excerpt: Artificial intelligence is driving significant transformation in drug discovery processes, representing a fundamental shift in how pharmaceutical innovation is conducted. This development reflects the growing integration of AI technologies into pharmaceutical research and development workflows. The paradigm shift encompasses new approaches to accelerating drug candidate identification and development timelines.

title: AI-Assisted Protein-Peptide Complex Prediction in a Practical Setting
excerpt: This study evaluates artificial intelligence methods for predicting protein-peptide complex structures in real-world applications. The research, conducted at the University of Pittsburgh's Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences, addresses the practical utility of AI-assisted approaches for modeling these molecular interactions.

title: The role of bioinformatics algorithms in modern biopharmaceutical design: Progress, challenges, and future perspectives
excerpt: Bioinformatics algorithms have become integral to modern biopharmaceutical design, enabling computational approaches to drug discovery and development. This review examines the current progress in applying these algorithms, identifies key technical and practical challenges in their implementation, and discusses emerging opportunities for advancement in the field.
